objective: Define and execute the full critical path required to make the current M365 capability commercially honest, operationally supportable, technically hardened, standards-compliant, and enterprise-ready as a standalone deterministic module.
decision_rule: Enterprise readiness requires product boundary lock, runtime hardening, identity-execution architecture, digital-employee operating-model lock, multi-executor runtime implementation, repo-validation closure, app-registration role separation, executor credential finalization, live-tenant certification, release decision closure, and launch readiness. Documentation alone is not sufficient.
prompt_discipline: Every execution act in this plan must have one formal MATHS prompt pair under docs/prompts/. Historical commercialization prompts remain traceability artifacts only.

completed.summary: Imported the supported-surface and buyer/operator boundary work from P0A and P0B.
Imported the canonical config-contract and auth/secret posture work from P1A and P1B.
Imported the governance, audit, live-validation, and release-gate definition work from P2A, P2B, P3A, and P3B.
Imported the packaging, onboarding, runbooks, and support-boundary work from P4A and P4B.
Made tenant-scoped config the runtime authority and added targeted config-authority verification.
Hardened fail-closed permission, approval, and policy behavior for the active ops-adapter path.
Replaced snapshot-mode admin audit with append-only admin event evidence and aligned admin dispatch with the registry contract.
Corrected the active master plan and governance template to match AGENTS.md, created the required project-file-index baseline, and made the active critical path explicit.
Regenerated the active MATHS prompt inventory so the master plan and every execution act A1 through D2 now have one formal prompt pair.

Removed the Black parse blocker in scripts/generate-policies.py, corrected the malformed governance invariants, and proved the narrow recovery surface with targeted parser, YAML, and formatter checks.
Restored the worktree to the approved post-B4C baseline, pinned the remaining Ruff/Black/Mypy inventory in artifacts/b4d1_failure_inventory.json, and sequenced B4D2 as the next bounded cleanup act.

Added notebook-backed L8 traceability for the scripts and CI tooling surface, eliminated the scoped Ruff debt under scripts/ and scripts/ci/, and validated the bounded cleanup with Ruff, py_compile, and diff checks before advancing to B4D3.

Added notebook-backed L9 traceability for the runtime, CLI, and notebook validation surface, eliminated the remaining targeted Ruff debt under src/ops_adapter/, src/provisioning_api/, src/smarthaus_cli/, and tests/test_policies.py, fixed the notebook formatter parse blocker in notebooks/ma_m365_batch1_groups.ipynb, stabilized targeted formatting, and validated the bounded cleanup with Ruff, ruff format --check, and git diff --check before advancing to B4D4.

Added types-PyYAML to the governed mypy hook and the repo dev dependencies, removed duplicate module discovery for src/ops_adapter/actions.py, and converted the governed mypy path from environment blockers into a real actionable type inventory.

Reduced the bounded core mypy surface from 90 errors across 11 files to green by tightening the runtime typing surfaces under src/ops_adapter/, src/smarthaus_common/, and src/smarthaus_graph/, correcting the duplicated CLI analyzer Path and signature issues under src/smarthaus_cli/, and validating the governed surface with targeted mypy, py_compile, and git diff --check.

Reduced the remaining dashboard, script, and test mypy surface from 126 errors across 15 files to green by adding bounded annotations and type-shape corrections under src/provisioning_api/, fixing the dynamic GraphClient typing and nullable group-id flow in the standalone scripts under scripts/, and validating the full bounded surface with targeted mypy and git diff --check.

Proved repo-wide mypy repeatability by running the governed pre-commit mypy all-files surface twice, confirming the same 67-error / 21-file remainder both times, and capturing the stable handoff inventory in artifacts/b4d4d_failure_inventory.json.

Closed the bounded B4D validation surface with green targeted Ruff, formatter, Mypy, and diff checks, then captured the current B4E handoff inventory in artifacts/b4d5_validation_handoff.json with repo-wide format green, repo-wide Ruff reduced to 26 issues, and repo-wide mypy reduced to 5 errors across 5 files.

Cleared the repo-wide validation gate to green with passing Ruff, formatter, mypy, full pre-commit, and diff checks, and recorded the authoritative closure in artifacts/b4e_full_repo_validation_closure.json.

Locked the enterprise production identity model as Entra-authenticated SMARTHAUS users plus app-only Graph execution through the SMARTHAUS service principal, documented the actor-versus-executor split, and synchronized the commercialization and certification docs to that architecture.

Enforced JWT-backed actor identity on the governed ops-adapter execution path, aligned the legacy app path to deny raw header-only actor access unless the explicit non-enterprise override is enabled, propagated the validated actor identity through the action request path, fixed the middleware fail-closed response boundary to return deterministic auth errors instead of leaking ASGI exceptions, and validated the bounded identity-enforcement surface with py_compile, targeted pytest, and diff checks.

Bound the authenticated SMARTHAUS Entra actor into tier resolution, approvals, and actor-versus-executor audit semantics by adding group-aware tier resolution, preserving actor tier, actor groups, tenant, and executor metadata in the governed runtime path, and validating the bounded binding surface with py_compile, targeted pytest, and diff checks.

Completed the governed Azure / Entra app-registration role-separation act by renaming app 720788ac-1485-4073-b0c8-1a6294819a87 to SMARTHAUS M365 Executor, renaming app e6fd71d3-4116-401e-a4f1-b2fda4318a8b to SMARTHAUS M365 Operator Identity, pruning Graph role overlap from the operator-identity app, pruning delegated-scope overlap from the executor app, and setting Application ID URI api://e6fd71d3-4116-401e-a4f1-b2fda4318a8b on the operator-identity app.

Completed the governed executor certificate-cutover act by fixing valid MSAL certificate-path handling in src/smarthaus_graph/client.py, generating the executor PEM under /Users/smarthaus/.ucp/certs/, appending the public certificate to app 720788ac-1485-4073-b0c8-1a6294819a87, updating the SMARTHAUS tenant contract to certificate-first auth with an empty client_secret, validating non-mutating Graph execution through the certificate path, and retiring the executor password credentials.

Locked the operator-facing digital-employee model in docs/commercialization/m365-digital-employee-operating-model.md and explicitly separated named SMARTHAUS personas from Microsoft executor identities.

Defined the capability, API, license, and auth matrix in docs/commercialization/m365-capability-api-license-auth-matrix.md so licensing scope, automation surface, and delegated-versus-app-only posture are no longer conflated.

Defined the bounded executor-domain split and minimum-permission model in docs/commercialization/m365-executor-domain-routing-and-minimum-permission-model.md, replacing the single over-broad executor posture with explicit domain executors.

Locked the persona-registry and humanized delegation contract in docs/commercialization/m365-persona-registry-and-humanized-delegation-contract.md, defining how named digital employees route through responsibilities, approvals, executor domains, and audit attribution.

Rebased certification in docs/commercialization/m365-certification-rebase-digital-employee-multi-executor-model.md so C1A no longer attempts to certify the legacy single-executor posture and now remains blocked until the multi-executor runtime exists.

Extended src/smarthaus_common/tenant_config.py so the tenant contract can represent bounded executors, executor-registry metadata, deterministic default projection, and fail-closed multi-executor default resolution.

Implemented deterministic action-to-executor routing in src/ops_adapter/actions.py, src/ops_adapter/main.py, src/ops_adapter/app.py, and src/ops_adapter/approvals.py, and validated the bounded SharePoint, directory, and approval-routing surface in tests/test_ops_adapter.py and tests/test_approvals.py.

Integrated a deterministic persona registry derived from registry/ai_team.json and registry/agents.yaml into src/ops_adapter/personas.py, src/ops_adapter/main.py, src/ops_adapter/app.py, and src/ops_adapter/approvals.py so named digital employees resolve to one canonical runtime agent, fail closed on inactive persona targets or bounded-domain mismatches, and preserve persona context through policy, approval, and audit payloads.

Read the live UCP constraint sources and evaluator, documented the exact accepted metadata contract for bounded test_run and governance-closeout validation shapes, and proved one bounded test_run shape plus one governance_edit shape with live validate_action so B7D can resume without undocumented gate behavior.

Created bounded SharePoint, collaboration, and directory executor apps with certificate-backed credentials, repaired the missing Graph app-role assignments for the SharePoint and directory service principals, demoted the old monolithic executor to SMARTHAUS Legacy M365 Executor, cut the active tenant contract over to the bounded SharePoint default, projected bounded executors into the supported v1 router and provisioning runtime surfaces, and re-proved live app-only SharePoint, collaboration, and directory access with exact bounded role sets and success responses.

Re-proved the approval backend through the bounded SharePoint executor under the exact standalone shell contract, returning 200 on both the pinned approvals list metadata route and the pinned approvals list items route without URL-based site discovery, and refreshed the C1A readiness packet to GO.

Closed the certification environment-readiness act because candidate 52ca494 and the refreshed evidence packet prove the SMARTHAUS tenant contract, bounded certificate-backed executors, pinned approvals target, and exact standalone shell contract are ready for live certification, and advanced the next act to C1B pending explicit live-execution approval.

Completed the live read-only certification act because list_users, get_user, list_teams, and list_sites all executed successfully against the live SMARTHAUS tenant through M365ConnectorModule, with the retained transcript in the certification packet.

Completed the live mutation and governance certification act by retaining the original live transcripts and closing the failed rows with bounded reproofs for provision_service, reset_user_password, the real actor-authenticated governed path on the supported OPA surface, approval create/readback, and audit evidence.

Completed the evidence-packet and matrix-closeout act by mapping the retained C1A through C1C packet into the standalone validation matrix and release-gate docs, and by adding the bounded validation_matrix_status artifact for candidate 52ca494.

Recorded the formal standalone release decision in artifacts/certification/m365-v1-candidate-52ca494/release_decision.json and sign_off_record.json, with an explicit GO because the required human engineering, security, and release-owner sign-off record is now complete.

prepared_blocked.summary: C2 is now complete with an explicit GO, D1 is the active next act, and D2 remains blocked until D1 is complete.

open_enterprise_blockers:
D1 remains open: the enterprise collateral pack is still required before launch-readiness closure.
D2 remains blocked until D1 is complete.

Persona Registry and Humanized Delegation Integration

critical_path_rules: No act after B4E may proceed while pre-commit run --all-files is red.
B5A may not start until B4E is complete.
B5D may not start until B5C is complete.
B5E may not start until B5D is complete.
B6A may not start until B5E is complete.
B7A may not start until B6E is complete.
B7D may not start until B7C1 is complete.
C1A may not start until B7E is complete.
C1B and C1C require explicit live-execution approval.
Historical commercialization prompts may not be used as active execution authority.
